sources: harpersbazaar.com (Fonts in Use, STUDIO 191 redesign docs)
Commercial Type, Terminal Design, Swiss Typefaces foundry pages
Brad Frost Conde Nast Style Guide

notes.accent: Vanity Fair's signature red — drop caps, kicker labels, active links, hover states
notes.surface: Vanity Fair cream/parchment for feature well backgrounds — the 'magazine paper' digital analogue
notes.border: Thin 1px warm-gray rules — section separators, column dividers

masthead.publicApproximation: Le Jeune (Commercial Type) or Cormorant Garamond
masthead.classification: Didone serif, high contrast, vertically stretched

headline.case: Title case for features; ALL CAPS for department headers

deck.typeface: VF Didot Italic or VF Sans

body.typeface: HTF Didot (long-form) or VF Sans (short pieces)

pullquote.border: Thin 1px horizontal rules above and below — NO left border
pullquote.attribution: Author name in VF Sans, small caps, below quote

byline.format: 'By' in italic serif + AUTHOR NAME in bold all-caps sans-serif

layout.heroPattern: Full-width image, headline BELOW on white — not overlaid. Strong 3:2 or 4:5 image ratio. Headline drops beneath image on clean white.
layout.featureSpread: Image left 60% + text right 40%, or alternating. Drop cap opens first paragraph. Byline above lead image.
layout.columnGrid: 12-col, 24px gutters. Article body 6–8 cols. Max-width 1280–1440px. Body text capped at ~660px.

components: Red all-caps kicker label above every headline (11px, 0.15em tracking)
Red drop cap opening feature articles (4–5 lines tall, Didone serif)
Pull quote flanked by thin 1px horizontal rules — no box border
Byline: italic 'By' + bold all-caps author name in sans
Section divider: single 1px warm-gray horizontal rule
Photo caption: 2–7 words max, muted gray, applied selectively
Featured story module: large image + stacked headline/deck/byline
Folio/issue label: small sans-serif in outer margin
